title: The Real Crusoe by Daniel Defoe | Explanation in Hindi | Class 6
description:
Before Daniel Defoe invented Robinson Crusoe, a real Scottish sailor named Alexander Selkirk had already survived four years alone on a deserted island — and Defoe turned that raw, terrifying truth into one of literature's greatest survival stories. "The Real Crusoe" brings that fascinating origin to Class 6 students, connecting themes of courage and self-reliance to a moment in history that actually happened. If you've ever wondered how a stranded man with almost nothing rebuilds an entire life from scratch, this chapter will hit differently once you know the true story behind it.
